# Pilot

> Cross-application browser OS with local AI

Important: Pilot runs entirely in the browser using WebLLM (local GPU) with Chrome APIs for context awareness.

## Docs
- [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): Runtime design and component interaction
- [Context Protocol](docs/context-protocol.md): How apps share context with Pilot
- [Tool Protocol](docs/tool-protocol.md): Defining and invoking tools
- [Chrome APIs](docs/chrome-apis.md): Browser integration reference

## Browser Runtime
- [Service Worker](browser/service-worker.ts): Central orchestrator
- [Content Script](browser/content-script.ts): Per-tab DOM observation
- [Context Aggregator](runtime/context-aggregator.ts): Browser state collection

## AI Runtime
- [WebLLM Engine](runtime/webllm-engine.ts): Local LLM inference
- [Chrome AI](runtime/chrome-ai.ts): Built-in AI fallback
- [Tool Router](runtime/tool-router.ts): Tool dispatch system

## SDK
- [Pilot Bridge](sdk/pilot-bridge.ts): Page ↔ Extension communication
- [Types](sdk/types.ts): Shared TypeScript definitions

## Optional
- [llms.txt Parser](sdk/llms-txt-parser.ts): Protocol implementation
